name: Cornell University
meets_full_need_notes: Cornell meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for domestic undergraduates. Need calculation uses CSS Profile and FAFSA. Aid policy varies slightly by college (endowed vs. contract/statutory colleges).
no_loan_notes: Cornell replaced loans with grants for families earning under $60,000 (announced 2023, Cornell Commitment expanded). Students with family income $60k-$120k see reduced loans. Above $120k, loans remain part of standard packages.

merit_thresholds.notes: Cornell offers limited merit scholarships including National Merit and Cornell-sponsored awards. Most institutional aid is need-based. Some colleges within Cornell have specific merit scholarship programs.

ed_aid_implications: ED applicants receive the same need-based aid calculations as RD applicants. Cornell is need-blind for domestic ED students. Aid offer released with admission decision.
special_programs: Cornell Commitment (enhanced aid for low-income students)
appeal_policy: Cornell has a formal appeals process through the Office of Financial Aid and Student Employment. Students submit appeals with documentation of changed circumstances.
